---
title: "Mouse forecast model evaluation"
subtitle: Autumn 2025
date: last-modified
format: 
  html:
    self-contained: true
    theme: cosmo
    fontcolor: black
    fig-width: 8  
    fig-asp: 0.8 # fig-height = fig-width * fig-asp
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
editor: source
toc: false
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| message: false
#| include: FALSE

## load packages
library(targets)
library(mvgam)

# no sci notation
options(scipen = 999)

## load model (as it is a file) 
model_path <- tar_read(forecast_model)  # returns the file path
model <- readRDS(model_path)   # loads the actual model object

```


```{r}
#| label: summary

# Display model summary neatly
summary_text <- capture.output(summary(model, smooth_test = FALSE))
cat(paste0("<pre style='font-size: 0.25em;'>", paste(summary_text, collapse = "\n"), "</pre>"))
```

```{r}
#| label: smooth_effects

# smooth and random effect plots from the process model
plot(model, "smooths", trend_effects = TRUE)
#plot(model, "re", trend_effects = TRUE)
```

```{r}
#| label: forecast_plots
#| results: 'asis'
#| fig-height: 4
#| fig-width: 8

# Retrieve the series names recorded in the fitted model and derive the count.
# levels() preserves the factor ordering used during model fitting.
series_names <- levels(model$obs_data$series)
n_series     <- length(series_names)

# Loop through every series and emit a heading followed by its forecast plot.
# results: 'asis' allows the cat() headings to be rendered as markdown while
# knitr captures each plot() call as a separate inline figure.
for (s in seq_len(n_series)) {
  cat(paste0("\n#### Series ", s, ": `", series_names[s], "`\n\n"))
  plot(model, type = "forecast", series = s,
       ylim = c(0, 1), ylab = "Relative abundance", xlab = "Season")
}
```
